rows.description: Computers Apel Rectors u 2d the Nov 1814 collected in dem woodhm - 1815
rows.notes: Opening title line, heavily abbreviated handwriting. Text is faded and difficult to parse with certainty. Appears to be a ledger header for 1814-1815. No amounts on this line.
Brief label with unclear handwriting. Appears to introduce a section. No amounts visible on this line.
Entry label, handwriting is cursive and faded. No amounts visible.

Handwriting is unclear but pattern shows '2' for pounds, '9' for shillings, and what appears to be '½' fraction symbol. Column alignment used reference from amounts on right margin (135, 136). Shillings and pence values are within valid ranges.
Heavily abbreviated, cursive handwriting is difficult to parse. Appears to show '2' for shillings only, but the full amount structure is unclear due to faded ink.
Text is faded and handwriting is cursive. Appears to be a place name or account label. No amounts visible on this line.

rows.description: Land of Redeemed Erington Rectory & Vicavage
rows.notes: Section header introducing a new account group. Handwriting is faded and difficult to read with certainty. No amounts on this line.
Subsection label. Handwriting is cursive and somewhat unclear. No amounts visible.

rows.description: St Newington P.T.S Haslington Mills 2d 2 Years
rows.notes: Multiple items listed on one line with associated amount on right margin (17.11). Reading from right-side amounts: 17 pounds, 11 shillings inferred. Column alignment is challenging due to dense handwriting but reference numbers on margin support this reading.
Amount visible on right margin (42.9.4½). Reading is clear from the annotated margin figure. All values within valid ranges.
Amount from right margin (10.4.4). Handwriting is cursive. All currency values within valid ranges.
Amount from right margin (9.11). Handwriting is faded but readable. Shillings value of 11 is within valid range.
Amount from right margin (8.-1). Handwriting is faded. The dash suggests zero shillings. All values within valid ranges.
Amount from right margin (44.2). Clear reading from margin annotation. All values within valid ranges.
Amount from right margin (28.7.4). Handwriting is very cursive and abbreviated. Values extracted from margin annotation are clear.
Amount from right margin (18.10.6). Handwriting is cursive and faded. All values within valid ranges.
Amount from right margin (12.4.10). Handwriting is cursive. All values within valid ranges.
Amount from right margin (2.12.0). Shillings value of 12 is at the boundary of valid range (should be 0-19). This appears to be correctly 12 shillings, but warrants verification.
Amount from right margin (24.3.10). Handwriting is cursive. All values within valid ranges.
Amount from right margin (26.12.6½). Shillings value of 12 is at boundary. The fraction symbol after pence appears to be 'ob' or similar mark for half-penny, interpreted as 0.5.
Section header introducing new account group. Handwriting is heavily abbreviated and faded. Text is difficult to parse with certainty. No amounts on this line.
Amount from right margin (8.0). Dash represents zero shillings. Handwriting is abbreviated.
Amount from right margin (6.-). Dash suggests zero or absent shillings and pence. Handwriting is abbreviated and faded.
Amount from right margin (1.10). Handwriting is cursive but readable from margin annotation.

Appears to be a subtotal or section total line labeled 'Receptha Anne presentes' (Receipt Anne presents). Amount from right margin (612.19.7). All values within valid ranges. Shillings of 19 is valid (max 19).

Amount from right margin (19.2.8). Brief entry with amounts. All values within valid ranges.
Amount from right margin (19.3.8). Similar brief entry. All values within valid ranges.
Final total line from right margin (38.7.4). Clear reading from underlined total at bottom of section. All values within valid ranges.
